chunk_summary: This section outlines criteria for inpatient discharge readiness and step-down handover in the management of severe acute malnutrition (SAM), emphasizing follow-up care and program quality to ensure sustained recovery and prevent relapse.
learning_objectives: Understand the criteria for inpatient discharge readiness in SAM management.
Learn the step-down handover process for transitioning patients to outpatient care.
Identify key follow-up measures to ensure program quality and sustained recovery.
Recognize the importance of monitoring and preventing relapse in SAM patients.
key_facts: Discharge readiness includes achieving target weight gain of ≥15% of admission weight.
Patients must demonstrate consistent appetite and ability to consume therapeutic foods independently.
Step-down care involves transitioning to outpatient follow-up with regular monitoring for at least 4 weeks.
SAM management guidelines emphasize the importance of caregiver education during discharge.
Relapse rates can be reduced by ensuring adherence to follow-up protocols and nutritional support.
reference_quote: Discharge readiness criteria include achieving a target weight gain of ≥15% of admission weight and demonstrating consistent appetite for therapeutic foods. (Source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, Operational guidelines on facility-based management of children with severe acute malnutrition, 2011)
document_key_topics: Inpatient discharge readiness criteria for SAM
Relapse prevention strategies in SAM management
